tree.setup: You're at a job interview for a marketing position at a Chinese company.
tree.setup_zh: 你在一家中国公司面试市场营销的职位。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 请先简单介绍一下你自己吧。
turns.text_pinyin: qǐng xiān jiǎndān jièshào yīxià nǐ zìjǐ ba.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Give a brief self-introduction relevant to the job.

options.text_zh: 我觉得可以去云南，那边风景好，而且五月天气不太热。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ juéde kěyǐ qù yúnnán, nàbiān fēngjǐng hǎo, érqiě wǔ yuè tiānqì bú tài rè.
options.text_en: I think we could go to Yunnan. The scenery is nice there, and the weather isn't too hot in May.
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to give a brief self-introduction relevant to the job, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

options.text_zh: 给您。请问大概需要多长时间？
options.text_pinyin: gěi nín. qǐngwèn dàgài xūyào duō cháng shíjiān?
options.text_en: Here you go. About how long will it take?
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to give a brief self-introduction relevant to the job, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

options.text_zh: 你好，我叫杰森，我大学学的是市场营销，毕业后有三年工作经验。
options.text_pinyin: nǐ hǎo, wǒ jiào jiésēn, wǒ dàxué xué de shì shìchǎng yíngxiāo, bìyè hòu yǒu sān nián gōngzuò jīngyàn.
options.text_en: Hello, my name is Jason, I studied marketing in university, and I have three years of work experience after graduation.
options.feedback: Structured and relevant — name, education, experience. Hits the key points for a professional intro.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 很好。你觉得你最大的优点是什么？
turns.text_pinyin: hěn hǎo. nǐ juéde nǐ zuì dà de yōudiǎn shì shénme?
turns.text_en: Very good. What do you think is your greatest strength?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Describe your greatest strength with an example.

options.text_zh: 我觉得我比较认真，做事之前会先做好计划，然后一步一步完成。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ juéde wǒ bǐjiào rènzhēn, zuòshì zhīqián huì xiān zuò hǎo jìhuà, ránhòu yī bù yī bù wánchéng.
options.text_en: I think I'm quite diligent — before doing things I make a plan first, then complete it step by step.
options.feedback: Concrete and believable — 认真 (diligent/serious) is highly valued in Chinese workplaces, and the example supports it.

options.text_zh: 我觉得可以去云南，那边风景好，而且五月天气不太热。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ juéde kěyǐ qù yúnnán, nàbiān fēngjǐng hǎo, érqiě wǔ yuè tiānqì bú tài rè.
options.text_en: I think we could go to Yunnan. The scenery is nice there, and the weather isn't too hot in May.
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to describe your greatest strength with an example, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

options.text_zh: 我们也很舍不得你。祝你在上海一切顺利，有空常联系。
options.text_pinyin: wǒmen yě hěn shěbude nǐ. zhù nǐ zài shànghǎi yīqiè shùnlì, yǒu kòng cháng liánxì.
options.text_en: We'll miss you too. Wish you all the best in Shanghai, keep in touch when you can.
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to describe your greatest strength with an example, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese job interviews, 认真 (rènzhēn, diligent/serious) and 踏实 (tāshi, down-to-earth/reliable) are among the most valued traits. Chinese interview culture tends to favor humility — using 比较 (bǐjiào, 'fairly/rather') softens self-praise. Saying 我最大的优点是... without hedging can sound arrogant.
